title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: Ten healthy adult participants performed a dual n-back task while continuous EEG was recorded. Working memory load was manipulated by varying n from 1 to 3. Accuracy declined and frontal theta power increased with increasing n-back load.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: We tested forward and backward digit span in a sample of 24 participants to assess verbal working memory capacity. Backward digit span correlated with performance on an unrelated reasoning task.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: Rodents were trained on a delayed match-to-sample task to probe short-term memory maintenance in prefrontal cortex. Optogenetic inhibition during the delay period impaired performance.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: Mice were trained in a Morris water maze over five days to assess spatial learning. Escape latency to the hidden platform decreased across training days in wild-type but not knockout animals.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: Participants navigated a virtual navigation task in a large-scale immersive environment while fMRI was recorded, allowing us to characterize grid-cell-like representations in entorhinal cortex.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: A y-maze spontaneous alternation task was used to assess working spatial memory in aged versus young mice; aged animals showed reduced alternation rates.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: Reaction times were measured during a visual search task with varying set sizes to assess attentional guidance by color and shape singletons.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: We used a flanker task to quantify interference effects and response inhibition in a sample of 40 young adults, and related performance to resting-state connectivity.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: An oddball task with auditory standard and deviant tones was used to elicit the mismatch negativity component while EEG was recorded from 18 participants.

title: Synthetic demo record — not a real PubMed entry.
abstract: Monkeys performed a random dot motion discrimination task while single-unit activity was recorded from area LIP, allowing us to relate ramping firing rates to accumulated sensory evidence.
